chapter_title.ar: باب مَوْلِدِ النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) وَ وَفَاتِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 111 – Nazool (Arrival) of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup> and his<sup>saww</sup> demise

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/253)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/253)
text: بَيْنَا النَّبِيُّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فِي الْمَسْجِدِ الْحَرَامِ وَ عَلَيْهِ ثِيَابٌ لَهُ جُدُدٌ فَأَلْقَى الْمُشْرِكُونَ عَلَيْهِ سَلَى نَاقَةٍ فَمَلَئُوا ثِيَابَهُ بِهَا فَدَخَلَهُ مِنْ ذَلِكَ مَا شَاءَ اللَّهُ فَذَهَبَ إِلَى أَبِي طَالِبٍ فَقَالَ لَهُ يَا عَمِّ كَيْفَ تَرَى حَسَبِي فِيكُمْ فَقَالَ لَهُ وَ مَا ذَاكَ يَا ابْنَ أَخِي فَأَخْبَرَهُ الْخَبَرَ

فَدَعَا أَبُو طَالِبٍ حَمْزَةَ وَ أَخَذَ السَّيْفَ وَ قَالَ لِحَمْزَةَ خُذِ السَّلَى ثُمَّ تَوَجَّهَ إِلَى الْقَوْمِ وَ النَّبِيُّ مَعَهُ فَأَتَى قُرَيْشاً وَ هُمْ حَوْلَ الْكَعْبَةِ فَلَمَّا رَأَوْهُ عَرَفُوا الشَّرَّ فِي وَجْهِهِ ثُمَّ قَالَ لِحَمْزَةَ أَمِرَّ السَّلى عَلَى سِبَالِهِمْ فَفَعَلَ ذَلِكَ حَتَّى أَتَى عَلَى آخِرِهِمْ ثُمَّ الْتَفَتَ أَبُو طَالِبٍ إِلَى النَّبِيِّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَقَالَ يَا ابْنَ أَخِي هَذَا حَسَبُكَ فِينَا .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hisham Bin Al Hakam,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘While the Prophet<sup>saww</sup> was in the Sacred Masjid and upon him<sup>saww</sup> were new clothes, so the Polytheists threw upon him<sup>saww</sup> the stomach contents of a she-camel. So his<sup>saww</sup> clothes were stained by it. So there entered into him<sup>saww</sup> due to that, whatever Allah<sup>azwj</sup> so Desired. So he<sup>saww</sup> went to Abu Talib<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> and said to him<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup>: ‘O Uncle! How do you<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> view my<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> nobility among you all?’ So he<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> said to him<sup>saww</sup>: ‘And what is that, O son<sup>saww</sup> of my<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> brother<sup>as</sup>?’ So he<sup>saww</sup> informed him<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> the news.

So Abu Talib<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> called Hamza<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> and grabbed the sword and said to Hamza<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> to take the stomach contents (of a camel), then divert towards the group, and the Prophet<sup>saww</sup> was with him<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup>. So he<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> came to the Qureysh, and they were around the Kabah. So when they saw him<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup>, they recognised the ferocity in his<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> face. Then he<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> said to Hamza<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup>: ‘Paste the stomach contents (of a camel) upon their faces’. So he<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> did that until he<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> came to their last one. Then Abu Talib<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> turned towards the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>, so he<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> said: ‘O son<sup>saww</sup> of my<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup> brother<sup>as</sup><sup>ws</sup>! This is your<sup>saww</sup> nobility among us’.
en.sarwar: 29. Al-Husayn ibn Muhammad and Muhammad ibn Yahya have narrated from Ahmad ibn Ishaq from Bakr ibn Muhammad al-Azdi from Ishaq ibn Ja‘far from his father (a.s.) who has said the following. “They think that abu Talib was an unbeliever. They speak lies, how could he be an unbeliever when he would say such worlds as herein below. “Do they not know that we found Muhammad as a prophet like Musa (Moses) whose name is written in the ancient books.” In another hadith he has said, “How could abu Talib be an unbeliever when he would say, “They certainly know that our child is not a liar to us and the false words are not paid any attention to. The (beautiful) white face that prays for rain from the clouds, is the helper of the orphans and the protector of the widows.”
